name: Vendor Risk Assessment
description: Third-party vendor risk assessment workflow that evaluates vendor security posture. When a new vendor assessment is initiated, the playbook collects the vendor's security questionnaire responses and external risk signals, uses Riggs AI to score the vendor against the organization's risk framework, and routes the decision based on risk tier. High-risk vendors require CISO approval; acceptable-risk vendors proceed to onboarding documentation.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Track vendor risk assessment lifecycle and approval workflow

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify procurement and security teams of vendor risk decisions

config.description: Manually triggered when a new vendor risk assessment is requested by procurement or business unit

config.query: SELECT q.question_category, q.question_text, q.vendor_response, q.evidence_provided, e.risk_signal, e.signal_source, e.signal_date FROM vendor_questionnaire q LEFT JOIN vendor_external_signals e ON q.vendor_id = e.vendor_id WHERE q.vendor_id = '{{trigger_1.vendor_id}}'
config.description: Collect vendor's security questionnaire responses and external risk intelligence signals

config.additional_instructions: Score this vendor against the organization's third-party risk framework. Evaluate security controls, data handling practices, incident response capability, compliance certifications, and external risk signals. Assign a risk tier (Low, Medium, High, Critical) and provide specific risk factors.
config.description: AI-powered vendor risk scoring and tier assignment
Routes high and critical risk vendors through additional approval; lower risk proceeds to documentation
CISO must approve or reject high-risk vendor engagement
Document the CISO's risk acceptance decision for the high-risk vendor
Create an onboarding ticket for the approved low/medium risk vendor
Notify procurement and security teams of the vendor assessment result
Vendor risk assessment complete -- scored, decided, and documented
